title: Phone English â€” calling, holding, transferring
description: Handle phone calls confidently â€” introducing yourself, asking to hold, transferring, leaving and taking messages.

content.title: Starting and ending a phone call
content.body:
STARTING a call â€” caller:
â€¢ 'Good morning, this is [name] calling from [company].'
â€¢ 'Hello, could I speak to [name], please?'
â€¢ 'Hi, I'm calling about [reason].'
â€¢ 'I'm calling to enquire about...'

STARTING â€” receiver:
â€¢ 'Good morning, [company name]. How may I help you?'
â€¢ '[Name] speaking â€” how can I help?'
â€¢ 'Hello, who am I speaking to?'

ENDING a call:
â€¢ 'Thank you for calling. Have a good day!'
â€¢ 'I'll look into that and get back to you.'
â€¢ 'Thanks â€” speak soon!'
â€¢ 'Goodbye!'
content.grammar_note:
'This is [name]' â€” NOT 'I am [name]' on phone calls
'Could I speak to...' â€” more polite than 'Give me'
Receiver: '[Name] speaking' = professional

content.title: Holding, transferring and taking messages
content.body:
ASKING TO HOLD:
â€¢ 'Could you hold the line, please?'
â€¢ 'Could you hold on a moment?'
â€¢ 'Please bear with me a moment.'
â€¢ 'Just a moment, please.'

TRANSFERRING:
â€¢ 'I'll transfer you to the right department.'
â€¢ 'Let me put you through to Ms Priya.'
â€¢ 'I'll connect you to the sales team.'

TAKING A MESSAGE:
â€¢ 'She's not available â€” can I take a message?'
â€¢ 'Could I take your name and number?'
â€¢ 'I'll make sure she gets this message.'

LEAVING A MESSAGE:
â€¢ 'Yes â€” could you tell her that [name] called?'
â€¢ 'Could you ask her to call me back?'
â€¢ 'My number is...'
content.grammar_note:
'Put you through' = transfer the call
'Bear with me' = please wait patiently
'I'll get back to you' = I'll call/email you later

vocabulary.example: Sorry â€” I think we got cut off!

vocabulary.meaning: when the signal is bad and voice is unclear
vocabulary.example: You're breaking up â€” can you hear me?
I'll call you back in five minutes.
I'll put you through to the manager.

vocabulary.meaning: a recorded message when someone doesn't answer
vocabulary.example: I left a voicemail â€” please call back.

sentences: Good morning â€” this is Arjun calling from TechBridge Solutions.
Could you hold the line for just a moment?
I'll put you through to the relevant department right away.
I'm afraid she's in a meeting â€” can I take a message?
Could you ask him to call me back at his earliest convenience?

content.question: You call a company. The receptionist answers. What do you say first?
options: I am Priya. Give me the manager.
Good morning â€” this is Priya Iyer calling. Could I speak to the HR manager, please?
Hi, it's me â€” is the manager there?
content.correct: Good morning â€” this is Priya Iyer calling. Could I speak to the HR manager, please?
content.explanation: 'This is [name] calling' + polite request = correct professional phone call opening.

content.question: The person you're calling is not available. The receptionist offers to take a message. What do you say?
options: Yes please â€” could you let her know that Ravi Kumar called and ask her to call me back on 98765 43210?
When will she be back? I'll call later.
content.correct: Yes please â€” could you let her know that Ravi Kumar called and ask her to call me back on 98765 43210?
content.explanation: A complete message: name + request to call back + number. Clear, polite, and professional.

content.question: During a call, the signal becomes very unclear. What do you say?
options: I'm sorry â€” you're breaking up a little. Could you repeat that?
content.correct: I'm sorry â€” you're breaking up a little. Could you repeat that?
content.explanation: 'Breaking up' = technical term for poor signal. Polite apology + polite request to repeat.

content.question: The caller asks for the accounts team. You need to transfer them. What do you say?
options: Accounts is not me. Hold on.
Let me put you through to the accounts team â€” please hold the line.
content.correct: Let me put you through to the accounts team â€” please hold the line.
content.explanation: 'Let me put you through' + 'please hold the line' = professional transfer language.

content.instruction: Fill in the correct phone English phrase.

sentences.text: 'Could you ___ the line for a moment?' (hold)
'I'm afraid she's not available. Can I ___ a message?' (record)
'Could you ___ me ___ at your earliest convenience?' (return my call)
'I'm sorry â€” we seem to have ___ ___.' (call disconnected)
sentences.answer: been cut off / got cut off

sentences.text: Caller: Is Mr Kapoor available? Agent: I'm afraid he's ___ a meeting right now.
Agent: Can I ___ a message? Caller: Yes please.
Caller: Could you ask him to ___ me ___ when he's free?
Agent: Of course â€” could I take your ___? Caller: Priya Sharma.

content.instruction: Arrange into a correct phone call opening.
content.answer: Good morning â€” this is Priya calling from TechCare.
Could you hold the line for just a moment?

content.instruction: Match the phone situation to the correct phrase.

pairs.left: The caller needs a different department
pairs.right: Let me put you through to the right team.

pairs.left: You can't hear the caller clearly
pairs.right: I'm sorry â€” you're breaking up. Could you repeat that?

pairs.left: The person they need is busy
pairs.right: I'm afraid she's in a meeting â€” can I take a message?

pairs.left: You need a moment to check something
pairs.right: Bear with me a moment â€” let me look that up.
Thank you for calling. Have a great day!

content.title: Extended phone call â€” job enquiry, Bangalore
content.setting: Arjun calls a company HR department to enquire about a job vacancy he saw online. This dialogue covers many phone call skills from this lesson.

dialogue.speaker: Receptionist
dialogue.text: Good afternoon â€” BrightTech Solutions, how may I help you?

dialogue.speaker: Arjun
dialogue.text: Good afternoon â€” this is Arjun Mehta calling. I'm calling to enquire about the Software Engineer vacancy advertised on your website.

dialogue.speaker: Receptionist
dialogue.text: Of course! Let me put you through to our HR department. Could you hold the line for a moment?

dialogue.speaker: Arjun
dialogue.text: Not at all, please go ahead.

dialogue.speaker: HR Agent
dialogue.text: HR department â€” Sonal speaking. How can I help?

dialogue.speaker: Arjun
dialogue.text: Good afternoon, Sonal. This is Arjun Mehta. I'm calling regarding the Software Engineer position posted on your website.

dialogue.speaker: HR Agent
dialogue.text: Oh yes â€” that's a current opening. Are you currently employed?

dialogue.speaker: Arjun
dialogue.text: Yes, I'm working with TechBridge â€” 4 years of experience in backend development.

dialogue.speaker: HR Agent
dialogue.text: Excellent. Bear with me a moment â€” let me pull up the job description to answer your questions better.

dialogue.speaker: Arjun
dialogue.text: Of course â€” take your time.

dialogue.speaker: HR Agent
dialogue.text: Right! So the role is based in Bangalore, with some travel. Do you have any specific questions?

dialogue.speaker: Arjun
dialogue.text: Could you tell me whether the role allows remote work on some days?

dialogue.speaker: HR Agent
dialogue.text: Yes â€” we have a hybrid model: 3 days in office, 2 remote. Anything else?

dialogue.speaker: Arjun
dialogue.text: And could you give me an idea of the application deadline?

dialogue.speaker: HR Agent
dialogue.text: Applications close on the 30th. I'd suggest emailing your CV to hr@brighttech.in â€” I'm afraid I can't accept it directly over the phone.

dialogue.speaker: Arjun
dialogue.text: Understood â€” I'll email it today. Thank you so much for your time, Sonal.

dialogue.speaker: HR Agent
dialogue.text: Our pleasure! Best of luck with your application. Goodbye!

comprehension_questions.question: Why is Arjun calling?
options: To resign from his current job
To enquire about a Software Engineer vacancy
comprehension_questions.correct: To enquire about a Software Engineer vacancy

comprehension_questions.question: What is the hybrid policy at BrightTech?
How does Arjun submit his application?

content.instruction: Find the phone call language mistake.

sentences.wrong: Hello, I am Priya. Is the manager?
sentences.correct: Good morning â€” this is Priya calling. Could I speak to the manager, please?
sentences.explanation: On a phone call: 'This is [name]' not 'I am'. Always add a polite request with 'could I speak to'.

sentences.wrong: Please hold, I'm transferring to you the accounts team.
sentences.correct: Please hold â€” I'll put you through to the accounts team.
sentences.explanation: 'Put you through to' is the correct phrase. 'Transferring to you' is incorrect word order.

sentences.wrong: She is not here. Leave message.
sentences.correct: I'm afraid she's not available right now. Can I take a message?
sentences.explanation: Professional phone language needs full sentences and polite phrasing.

content.scenario: You call a restaurant to book a table. The line is very noisy and you can barely hear the person. What do you say?
options: I'm sorry â€” I'm having a little difficulty hearing you. Could you speak up slightly?
content.correct: I'm sorry â€” I'm having a little difficulty hearing you. Could you speak up slightly?
content.explanation: Polite apology + polite request. Never shout instructions â€” always frame it as your difficulty.

content.instruction: Role-play a full phone call. You are calling a hotel to book a room for 3 nights. Use the phone language from this lesson throughout.
content.prompt: Make a hotel booking call covering: introduction, request, dates, room type, price, and confirmation.
content.example_response: Good evening â€” I'm calling to make a booking. This is Kavya Nair calling. I'd like to enquire about availability for the 15th to the 17th of October â€” that's three nights. Could you tell me what room types are available? Oh, a standard room and a deluxe â€” could you tell me the difference in price? The standard is â‚¹3,500 per night and the deluxe is â‚¹5,200? Could I book the standard room please? And could I confirm â€” does the price include breakfast? Wonderful â€” so that's breakfast included. Could you hold on a moment while I check one thing? Yes, I'm back â€” I'd like to proceed with the booking. Could you take my name â€” it's Kavya Nair, K-A-V-Y-A. My number is 98765 43210. And could you send a confirmation to kavya.nair@email.com? Perfect â€” thank you so much! Goodbye!

content.instruction: You are the receptionist at a company. A caller wants to speak to someone who is unavailable. Handle the call: take a message, ask for their name and number, and promise to pass it on.
content.prompt: Play the role of a professional receptionist handling an incoming call where the required person is busy.
content.example_response: Good morning â€” Sunrise Consulting, Arjun speaking. How may I help you? Good morning! I'm afraid Ms Sonal Mehta is in a meeting right now and unavailable to take calls. I expect she'll be free in about 30 minutes. Can I take a message for her? Of course â€” could I have your name please? Ms Priya Rajan â€” thank you. And your contact number? 98765 43210 â€” I'll note that. And could you tell me briefly what it's regarding so I can let her know? It's about the Q4 proposal â€” I'll make sure to mention that. Would you like her to call you back as soon as she's free, or is there a specific time that works better for you? Any time after 2 PM works? Perfect â€” I'll pass on the message. Thank you for calling, Ms Rajan â€” have a great day!
key_phrases: I'm afraid [name] is in a meeting
I'll make sure she gets this

content.instruction: Complete this phone message note form based on the call details.
content.text:
PHONE MESSAGE SLIP

Date: Monday 14th October
Time: 2:35 PM

Message ___ (1): Sonal Mehta
Called ___ (2): Priya Rajan
Company: Rajan Consultants
Phone: ___ (3): 98765 43210
Reason ___ (4) call: Regarding the Q4 marketing proposal

Action ___ (5): Please ___ (6) Ms Priya Rajan ___ (7) your earliest convenience.

Message ___ (8) by: Arjun (Reception)

content.title: Lesson 5 complete â€” Phone English
key_points: Caller opening: 'This is [name] calling from [company].' (not 'I am')
Receiver opening: '[Name] speaking â€” how can I help?'
Hold: 'Could you hold the line?' / 'Bear with me a moment.'
Message: 'Can I take a message?' + name + number + reason
Problems: 'You're breaking up' / 'We got cut off'
Ending: 'Thank you for calling. Have a great day!'
